sn45.158:1.0.1: "Bhikkhus, suppose there were a seafaring ship bound with rigging that had been worn out in the water for six months.It would be hauled up on dry land during the cold season and its rigging would be further attacked by wind and sun. Inundated by rain from a rain cloud, the rigging would easily collapse and rot away. So too, when a bhikkhu develops and cultivates the Noble Eightfold Path, his fetters easily collapse and rot away.
